perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector in Inside() with BVH triangle ray-cast (#277)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector in Inside() with BVH triangle ray-cast

Replace the per-face IntCurvesFace_Intersector calls for curved faces in
G4OCCTSolid::Inside() with a single BVH traversal of the pre-built fTriangleSet
using Moller-Trumbore ray-triangle intersection.

Previously, every curved face invoked IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform()
individually. For a TwistedTubs solid with many curved faces, this resulted in
N_curved_faces expensive OCCT solver calls per Inside() query.

The new TriangleRayCast BVH traversal class casts the same +Z ray through the
BVH-accelerated triangle mesh (fTriangleSet) in a single O(log N_triangles)
traversal, counting crossings via Moller-Trumbore. Degenerate hits (ray near
triangle edge/vertex) fall back to BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier as before.
When fTriangleSet is unavailable, the original per-face path is retained as a
fallback.

Observed speedup in test suite wall-clock time: ~430 s -> ~62 s (7x) for the
full geometry validation run including TwistedTubs and ellipsoid fixtures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden TriangleRayCast and Inside() Tier-2a BVH path per review (#284)

* Initial plan

* fix: apply review feedback to TriangleRayCast and Inside() BVH path

- Reset myCrossings/myOnSurface/myDegenerate in SetRay() so each cast
  starts from a clean state (prevents misuse on reuse)
- Replace 1e100/1e-12 sentinels in RejectNode() with Precision::Infinite()

- Add deflection-band guard in Tier-2a: fall back to exact classifier

fix: update timer phases after calibration (Update button) (#232)

In handleUpdate, updatePhases() was called synchronously immediately after
ref.calibrate(). The calibrate() implementations in Gen3/Gen4/Gen5Panel
update Zustand state, but their createDisplayData callbacks are captured via
useCallback and still hold the previous store values in their closure. React
hasn't re-rendered yet, so createDisplayData computed phases from the old
(pre-calibration) settings and the timer display never reflected the change.

The workaround users found (manually editing a calibration field) happened
to work because those edits go through the update() helper which already
uses setTimeout(onPhasesChange, 0), giving React one event-loop tick to
re-render with the new Zustand values before createDisplayData is called.

Fix: use setTimeout(updatePhases, 0) in handleUpdate — consistent with
every other call site (handleReset, handleSettingsClose, initial useEffect,
and all field-level update() helpers in every panel).

Fixes #230

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: batched sensitivity analysis with jax.vmap (#153)

Add vmap-based batched energy evaluation to JAX engines so that
compute_sensitivity() can evaluate all 2N+1 parameter perturbations
in a single vectorized call instead of sequentially.

Engine layer:
- MMEngine base: supports_batched_energy() / batched_energy() with
  sequential fallback for non-JAX backends
- JaxEngine: vmap over _energy_fn with cached JIT compilation
- JaxMDEngine: vmap over _scalar_energy_fn, broadcasting coords
  and neighbor list

Objective layer:
- is_energy_only(): detect pure-energy reference sets
- batched_scores(): dispatch to batched or sequential path
- _batched_energy_scores(): vectorized residual computation

Cycling layer:
- compute_sensitivity() builds (2N+1, P) param matrix upfront
  and calls batched_scores() for the entire sensitivity sweep

Tests:
- 5 new JAX-specific tests covering batched_energy, is_energy_only,
  batched_scores consistency, and end-to-end compute_sensitivity

commit.message:
feat: add multi-hop routing, broadcast propagation, delivery ACK, buffer utilization, and stop cleanup — multi-hop TDD (8 cycles, 89 tests)

Multi-hop integration:
- 3-node end-to-end: Alice→Bob→Charlie via routing table relay
- Routed relay uses bestRoute() for non-direct neighbors
- Destination sends TYPE_DELIVERY_ACK back to origin on routed message receipt
- Broadcast propagates A→B→C via re-flooding
- Broadcast with maxHops=0 delivers locally only, no re-flood
- hopLimit=0 on relay drops message (not forwarded)

Fixes and improvements:
- meshHealth() calculates real buffer utilization from outbound + reassembly bytes
- stop() clears presenceTracker, pauseQueue; restart gives clean slate
- PresenceTracker: added clear() and connectedPeerIds() methods
- DeliveryTracker: added isTracked() method

commit.message:
feat: wire broadcast, routed message, delivery ACK, routing table, presence tracker, and diagnostics into MeshLink — wire format integration TDD (8 cycles, 81 tests)

Wire format handlers:
- TYPE_BROADCAST: deliver to self, re-flood with decremented hops, dedup
- TYPE_ROUTED_MESSAGE: deliver if destination, relay via next-hop if not
- TYPE_DELIVERY_ACK: emit via deliveryConfirmations Flow

Component integration:
- DeliveryTracker: exactly-once terminal signals, duplicate ACK rejected
- DiagnosticSink: emit RATE_LIMIT_HIT and PEER_EVICTED, drainDiagnostics() API
- PresenceTracker: replaces knownPeers set, sweep-based 2-miss eviction
- RoutingTable: send() to non-neighbor routes via bestRoute as routed message

New public API:
- broadcast(payload, maxHops): flood to all neighbors
- sweep(seenPeers): presence sweep returning evicted peer IDs
- addRoute(destination, nextHop, cost, seqNum): manual route injection
- drainDiagnostics(): poll diagnostic events
- BleTransport.localPeerId: exposed for self-send and routing

commit.message:
ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job (#290)

* ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job

The three callgrind profile jobs each ran a full CMake configure+build
before profiling. The matrix job rebuilt the same bench_callgrind binary
seven times (one per fixture), even though the fixture is only a runtime
argument.

Add a new build-reldebinfo job that:
- Configures with RelWithDebInfo, BUILD_BENCHMARKS=ON, BUILD_EXAMPLES=ON,
  ENABLE_WERROR=ON, B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF, and VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR.
- Builds all targets in one pass.
- Uploads bench_callgrind, bench_assembly_navigator, and exampleB4c as
  the reldebinfo-binaries artifact (retention 1 day).

All three callgrind jobs now declare needs: [build-reldebinfo], download
the artifact, and run only the profile + annotate steps inside eic_xl.

Also add -DB4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF to build-test-benchmark. This flag
only affects exampleB4c behavior and is safe to set globally.

commit.message:
Suggest remoteBuild when Docker is missing (#7247)

* Suggest remoteBuild when Docker is missing (#7240, #5715)

When Docker is not installed or not running, azd now suggests
'remoteBuild: true' as an alternative for services that support it
(Container Apps, AKS). This addresses 2,936 Docker.missing failures
and 4,655 Docker.failed errors per 28 days.

Two-layer approach:

1. error_suggestions.yaml: Added catch-all rule matching
   MissingToolErrors with 'Docker' pattern, suggesting remoteBuild
   with install links. Covers all error paths as a safety net.

2. EnsureServiceTargetTools: Enhanced with service-aware suggestion.
   When Docker is missing, inspects which services actually need it
   (excluding dotnet-publish services that bypass Docker) and returns
   a targeted ErrorWithSuggestion listing the specific services that
   can use remoteBuild.

Fixes #7240
Fixes #5715

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: add Podman install link to Docker suggestion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: differentiate not-installed vs not-running, add Podman

Per @weikanglim feedback:
- Distinguish 'not installed' from 'not running' with different suggestions
  (start runtime vs install Docker/Podman)
- Mention Podman alongside Docker in both Go suggestion and YAML rules
- Split YAML rule into two: 'not running' (matched first) and 'not installed'
- Add test cases for both scenarios

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix line length lint (125 char max)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: use service target tool requirements instead of host assumptions

Per @vhvb1989 feedback: project_manager should not assume which targets
support remoteBuild. Instead, check whether the service target's
RequiredExternalTools actually listed Docker, which respects custom
service targets from extensions.

The suggestRemoteBuild helper now takes svcToolInfo (which services
needed Docker per their target) instead of checking Host.RequiresContainer().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

bench: add NIST CTC timing benchmarks and point cloud generation (#292)

Replace the unconditional `continue` for IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture
(NIST CTC) with reduced-ray-count BM_rays and BM_inside benchmarks.
Using kNistCtcRayCount=64 rays avoids timeouts on large AP203 compound
assemblies while producing timing data and point cloud output.

mismatches are always 0.  The benchmarks wrap CompareFixtureRays in a
try-catch so a G4Exception on a pathological CTC geometry does not abort
the entire benchmark run.

fix: strip UTF-8 BOM from funcignore and fix negation pattern handling (#7311)

* fix: strip UTF-8 BOM from funcignore and fix negation pattern handling

The go-gitignore library does not handle UTF-8 BOM (byte order mark)
that some Windows editors (e.g. Notepad) prepend to files. When a
.funcignore file has a BOM, the invisible prefix bytes become part of
the first pattern, causing it to never match. This broke the
node_modules exclusion detection in resolveFunctionAppRemoteBuild,
leading to incorrect remoteBuild validation results.

Changes:
- Strip UTF-8 BOM from .funcignore content before parsing in both
  resolveFunctionAppRemoteBuild (validation) and createDeployableZip
  (packaging) for consistent behavior
- Switch createDeployableZip from gitignore.NewFromFile to manual
  read + gitignore.New for consistency with validation code path
- Fix negation pattern handling in createDeployableZip: check
  match.Ignore() instead of just match != nil, so negation patterns
  (e.g. !node_modules/important) correctly include files
- Add comprehensive BOM-handling tests and stripUTF8BOM unit tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clean up temp zip file on ignore file read error

Close and remove the temporary zip file when reading the ignore file
fails with a non-NotExist error, preventing file handle leaks and
orphaned temp files (especially on Windows).

commit.message:
Add OWL ontology tutorial page with embedded live demos

New files:
- packages/web/public/owl-tutorial.html
  Full step-by-step tutorial: start from template → add ontology
  header → place classes → build hierarchy → add instances →
  add object properties → run auto-layout → export to Turtle.
  Each section has prose instructions and an embedded live BoxesEditor
  iframe pre-loaded with that stage of the Animals ontology.
  Includes a highlighted Turtle code block for the export step,
  a sticky TOC with IntersectionObserver active-section tracking,
  and a responsive dark-theme layout.

- packages/web/public/demo.html + demo.js
  Thin iframe-friendly BoxesEditor wrapper.
  Params: ?graph=<url> to load a .boxes file, ?tab=<id> to activate
  a sidebar tab, ?sidebar=0 to hide the sidebar for canvas-only views.

- packages/web/public/demos/owl-{1..7}-*.boxes
  Seven incremental demo graphs for the tutorial iframes, each a
  valid .boxes file (OWL palette + stylesheet + Animals ontology
  elements at preset positions):
    1-empty      — empty canvas, palette visible
    2-ontology   — owl:Ontology header node
    3-classes    — 7 owl:Class nodes, no edges yet
    4-hierarchy  — subClassOf edges connected
    5-instances  — individuals typed via rdf:type
    6-properties — owl:ObjectProperty + Person class
    7-complete   — full graph ready for layout

Modified:
- packages/core/src/boxes-editor.js
  Add public setActiveTab(tabId) method so demo.js can activate
  a specific sidebar tab programmatically.

- packages/web/public/index.html
  Add 'Building an OWL Ontology' tutorial link on welcome screen.

- packages/web/vite.config.js
  Add demo.html + owl-tutorial.html as Rollup entry points.
  Add copyDemoFiles() plugin to copy demos/*.boxes → dist/demos/
  during production build.
